message:
feat(snapshot_engine): Cx-first cleanup_with_cx entry point (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Adds SnapshotEngine::cleanup_with_cx behind the asupersync-runtime
feature, extending the Cx-first migration pattern already landed for
shutdown_checkpoint_with_cx (commit 6de7d826) to the retention cleanup
path. Mirrors the session_retention::cleanup_sessions_async_cx idiom:
checkpoint gates around the spawn_blocking handoff so canceled callers
skip the blocking DB work / stale result propagation.

Scope:

1. crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs (+57 source lines,
   additions only, plus 120 test lines)

   Adds `SnapshotEngine::cleanup_with_cx(cx: &Cx) -> Result<usize, _>`.

     * `cleanup_runs` counter increments *before* the pre-cancel
       check, preserving observability parity with the legacy
       `cleanup` — operators can still see how many cleanup
       *attempts* the engine saw even if the caller canceled pre-work.

     * `cx.checkpoint()` before `spawn_blocking` short-circuits to
       `Ok(0)` if the caller has already abandoned the operation.
       The DB is untouched.

     * `cx.checkpoint()` after the blocking work returns. A
       late-canceled caller is surfaced `Ok(0)` rather than the
       stale removed count so the wider call graph does not act on
       a result the caller has disowned. The actual DB removal
       already happened (and `cleanup_removed` reflects it); only
       the return value is suppressed.

   Legacy `cleanup` preserved; strictly additive.

   Two new tests:

     * `cleanup_with_cx_pre_cancelled_skips_db_work`: plants 4
       checkpoints with retention_count=2 (legacy cleanup would
       remove 2), then pre-cancels the Cx and calls cleanup_with_cx.
       Asserts (a) return value is Ok(0), (b) all 4 checkpoints
       still exist in the DB, (c) cleanup_runs counter incremented
       by 1, (d) cleanup_removed counter unchanged.

     * `cleanup_with_cx_happy_path_removes_overflow`: no-regression
       check that cleanup_with_cx with a fresh Cx removes the
       retention overflow like `cleanup` does.

Verification (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):

  * cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib snapshot_engine::: 87/87 pass
    (85 existing + 2 new Cx-first)
  * cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --lib -D warnings: clean
  * rustfmt --check: clean

SnapshotEngine now has both Cx-first entry points that matter for the
goal-line push: shutdown_checkpoint_with_cx (emergency snapshot path)
and cleanup_with_cx (retention path). run_periodic is the remaining
legacy-path method on this type but it's a much larger refactor
(dispatches across two scheduling modes) so out of scope here.

Leaves ft-xbnl0.2.3 still in_progress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
